meta.title: Unit 1 — Singular & Plural Nouns (Test 7)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Reorder sentences and identify correct plurals with -s and -es rules.

questions.question: What is the plural of "rock"?
questions.explanation: rock → rocks. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: The plural of "tree" is "trees".
questions.explanation: Yes! tree → trees. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Write the plural of "car".
questions.explanation: car → cars. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Three ___ are by the tree. (rock)
questions.explanation: "Three" tells us we need plural: rocks.

questions.question: The plural of "beach" is "beachs".
questions.explanation: beach ends in -ch, so we add -es: beach → beaches.

questions.question: Write the plural of "bird".
questions.explanation: bird → birds. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Which sentence uses the correct plural?
options: Three rock are by the tree.
Three rocks are by the tree.
Three roces are by the tree.
Three rockies are by the tree.
questions.explanation: rock → rocks. "Three" tells us we need plural.

questions.question: Sort these words: Singular or Plural?
questions.explanation: rock/car/beach = one (singular). trees/birds = more than one (plural).

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "There are many stars in the sky."

questions.question: In the sentence "Six bugs are on the rocks," both "bugs" and "rocks" are plural nouns.
questions.explanation: Yes! bugs (plural of bug) and rocks (plural of rock) are both plural.

questions.question: Write the plural of "beach".
questions.explanation: beach → beaches. Words ending in -ch add -es.

questions.question: Which noun does NOT need -es to be plural?
questions.explanation: tree → trees (just add -s). The others need -es.

questions.question: Sort these plurals: did they add -s or -es?
questions.explanation: Regular nouns add -s. Nouns ending in -ch or -sh add -es.

questions.question: The plural of "wish" is "wishs".
questions.explanation: wish ends in -sh, so we add -es: wish → wishes.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "I see five cars outside."

questions.question: Write the plural of "branch".
questions.explanation: branch → branches. Words ending in -ch add -es.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I plant two bush in the yard.
I plant two bushs in the yard.
I plant two bushes in the yard.
I plant two bushies in the yard.
questions.explanation: bush → bushes (add -es). "two" tells us we need plural.

questions.question: Which word ends in -ch and adds -es to become plural?
questions.explanation: watch ends in -ch, so watch → watches (add -es).

questions.question: In the sentence "She has one box," changing to 'three' means the sentence becomes 'She has three box.'
questions.explanation: "three" means plural: She has three boxes (box → boxes, add -es).

questions.question: Write the plural of "log".
questions.explanation: log → logs. Regular nouns add -s.
